description: A 'kowela' is a continuing collective body that deliberates or takes decisions for a stated community or purpose. It is more durable than 'lona' (meeting), which names one gathering. A council may use consensus, voting, or another procedure; its name supplies none of them automatically.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into true-mid rounded 'o'. Round the lips for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into stressed true-mid 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l', let air pass along its sides, and finish on central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'ko' opens into the lip glide and clearer centre of stressed 'we', then lateral 'la' lets the sound move out into open 'a'. The three releases give the word a measured, gathering rhythm without making any claim about how the council behaves.
usage_notes: Councils count individually, and the community or purpose commonly appears before the noun. A council can hold a meeting, adopt a procedure, or claim authority, each in its own clause. The title of a particular council remains exact source material or a proper name accepted by that body.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A council gives shared land, infrastructure, and care a continuing place for deliberation beyond one meeting.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The neutral noun leaves participation, listening, dissent, and decision method visible instead of praising the body merely for calling itself a council.

semantic_domains.communication: a continuing body in which collective deliberation may occur
semantic_domains.community: a collective body organised around a community or shared purpose
